vocab.meaning: chi một khoản tiền lớn (thường là miễn cưỡng)
vocab.example: I had to fork out a significant amount for those limited-edition sneakers, but they were worth every penny.

vocab.meaning: mua nhanh chóng (thường vì giá rẻ hoặc hàng hiếm)
vocab.example: When I saw the clearance sale online, I immediately snapped up two pairs of running shoes.

vocab.meaning: thử đồ (để xem có vừa hoặc hợp không)
vocab.example: The biggest drawback of e-commerce is that you can't try on the shoes to check the fit before purchasing.

vocab.meaning: gửi trả lại (hàng hóa không ưng ý)
vocab.example: The loafers I ordered were a bit too tight, so I had to send them back and request a larger size.

vocab.meaning: lục tìm kỹ lưỡng, rà soát
vocab.example: I spent hours scouring through various retail websites to find a specific pair of vintage boots.
By the time I decided to buy the trainers, they had completely sold out in my size.

vocab.meaning: chờ đợi, canh chừng (một đợt giảm giá hoặc sản phẩm mới)
vocab.example: I always look out for discount codes or seasonal vouchers before I finalize any online footwear purchase.

vocab.meaning: đảm bảo cho, xác nhận chất lượng cho
vocab.example: I only shop from websites that my friends can vouch for, especially regarding the accuracy of their size charts.

vocab.meaning: tìm thấy sau một quá trình tìm kiếm khó khăn
vocab.example: It took me weeks, but I finally managed to track down a rare pair of heels on a niche resale platform.

vocab.meaning: phù hợp với, đi kèm với (quần áo/phong cách)
vocab.example: I bought these white sneakers online specifically because they go with almost everything in my wardrobe.
